1. Backflow

GO / active sourcing. Buy a renewal database disguised as a small field-service company.

2. Water Filtration

WATCH-to-GO. Works only as an installed-base service route with recurring salt/filter/service revenue.

Buyer thesis30-name trackerSprint memo

Two arenas, not one blended list.

Arena 1

Services

Recurring local service businesses: route density, customer books, skilled field labor, compliance/maintenance, home and property systems.

Default posture: buy first, franchise second, DIY only selectively.

RoutesTechniciansReviewsCustomer files
Arena 2

Multi-unit

Repeatable consumer-facing units: food, retail, education, wellness, specialty trade retail, membership concepts.

Default posture: DIY first where testable, franchise where system value is real, acquisition/resale selectively.

BrandSite selectionUnit economicsPlaybook creation

The 2×3 decision matrix.

DIY / build
Franchise
Acquisition / buy
Services
Selective only

Works for simple, pre-sellable routes: mosquito, soft wash, holiday lighting, maybe irrigation if a field lead exists.

Must prove: demand before labor.

Can work

Use when SOPs, territory, training, insurance/compliance, or lead gen are worth royalties.

Risk: paying for a logo.

Best fit

Buy customer books, techs, licenses, routes, reviews, and cash flow. Then modernize.

Risk: owner-dependent job.

Multi-unit
Best fit

Best when brand, product, content, community, and first-unit playbook are the upside.

Examples: candy, kids STEM, sports academy.

Selective

Use only if training, sourcing, curriculum, site selection, or benchmarking changes the odds.

Example: Metal Supermarkets.

Selective

Buy resales when reviews, lease/site, staff, or provider base matter more than concept creation.

Example: medspa.

Current strategic take.

Definitive 2026-06-07 reset: mandated recurring routes first; memo-backed validation second; nascent 2030 ideas third; discretionary consumer concepts last. Trend strength can create a memo candidate, but it no longer outranks deal-flow proof, recurring mandated demand, and Dan-fit acquisition feasibility.

Active Sourcing Spine

Backflow, mosquito/tick, water filtration, AIM Act refrigeration, grease trap, hood cleaning, weatherization, septic, lead paint, and insurance books. Small, boring, recurring, mandated or renewal-driven, buyable, and fragmented.

Consumer Porting Sprint

Premium bagels, pet hygiene membership, and party-first kids/family remain consumer lanes worth visualizing and diligence-testing after the June 7 reset. Open the consumer porting page.

Demoted / Removed

Fire/life-safety, car wash, auto repair, mold remediation, RCM, tutoring, boba, generic coffee, mochi/Asian bakery, salad, pilates/barre, pet waste, and zero-score placeholders are no longer allowed to pollute the default top board.

Full category universe.

Full ranked list across Services and Multi-unit. Default view is Dan shortlist: non-killed, Northeast/Miami-viable, recurring/compliance weighted, and memo-backed. Switch to Full 306 when you want the long tail. Path fit = how feasible the best execution route is. Open any row for economic detail, proof points, memo links, and kill tests.
